lessons.context: Python \b regex corrupts Myanmar compound words
lessons.lesson: Python re module \b treats Myanmar combining marks (Unicode Mn category, e.g. ိ U+102D, ု U+102F) as \W, creating false word boundaries INSIDE syllables. regex \bထို\b matches inside compounds like ထိုင်ခိုင်း → အဲဒီင်ခိုင်း. NEVER use \b for Myanmar text.
lessons.action: Use Myanmar-consonant-specific lookahead/lookbehind: (?<![က-အ])WORD(?!combining*[က-အ]). Also add undo function for pre-existing corruptions.

lessons.context: BOM (U+FEFF) not stripped by Python str.strip()
lessons.lesson: str.strip() only removes whitespace characters. BOM (U+FEFF, Zero Width No-Break Space) is NOT whitespace. Files written with UTF-8-SIG encoding have BOM that breaks heading detection with startswith('#') and regex ^ anchor.
lessons.action: Always .lstrip('\ufeff') or .replace('\ufeff', '') before processing heading lines.

lessons.lesson: padauk-gemma can output Tamil (and potentially other Indic scripts) mixed in Myanmar text. Postprocessor must strip ALL non-Myanmar scripts, not just Chinese/Bengali/Thai.
lessons.action: Added _INDIC_PATTERN covering 9 Indic script blocks. Always strip in clean_output().

lessons.context: Novel: Reverend Insanity — padauk-gemma glossary comparison garbage
lessons.lesson: padauk-gemma at temperature ≥ 0.4 outputs word-by-word glossary comparison junk (*:* "word" is . "other" is .) mixed with translation. These are NOT caught by existing reasoning-stripping patterns. Temperature 0.2 prevents this behavior.
lessons.action: Always use temperature ≤ 0.2 for padauk-gemma. Add *:* pattern detection in postprocessor as defense-in-depth.

lessons.context: Duplicate chapter headings re-appeared after postprocessor fix
lessons.lesson: remove_duplicate_headings() used exact string match but the padauk-gemma model outputs slightly different heading text per chunk (e.g., နှင်းမှုန် vs နှင်းစက်). Prefix matching on '# အခန်း N' pattern is required.
lessons.action: Always use prefix/regex matching for heading deduplication, not exact string comparison.

lessons.lesson: .agent/ directory is the agent brain — must be created before any other operation. All files follow strict schemas defined in AGENTS.md.
lessons.action: Always create .agent/ files with correct schemas on first session

known_patterns.cause: .agent/ directory is not committed to version control (contains session state)
known_patterns.fix: Auto-create .agent/ files from AGENTS.md schema on first run

padauk-gemma:q8_0.notes: Primary EN→MM model. 98%+ Myanmar ratio. Best results with temp=0.2, repeat_penalty=1.15. DO NOT increase temperature above 0.2 — causes glossary comparison garbage (*:* pattern).

alibayram/hunyuan:7b.notes: CN→EN pivot Stage 1 model. Good Chinese comprehension.

qwen:7b.notes: Fast checker model. Produces English not Myanmar — do NOT use as translator. Only suitable for validation tasks.

qwen2.5:14b.notes: Good Chinese comprehension but outputs Japanese/Chinese instead of Myanmar. Use only for CN→EN pivot, not for Myanmar output.

burmese-gpt:7b.notes: Registered GGUF model via Modelfile.burmese-gpt. Not yet production tested.
